topic: Telluric ~12 Hz peak — change-detected verification of ISS-1993 patch (harmonic-spectrum enumeration added to detail_evidence)
change_summary: detail_evidence grew from 850 to 1052 chars (+23.8%); single sentence added at end: 'Measured Schumann harmonics span 7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, and 33.8 Hz; all four harmonics above the fundamental fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula, and none corresponds to 11.7 Hz.' This is the ISS-1993 patch (closed 2026-05-14 via EXP-369 P0), addressing c4 carry-over flagged by c5 hole #2 — quantitative harmonic enumeration was missing from detail_evidence and required cross-link to WIN-008 for standalone readability. Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-24T05:42:10Z; obe_skipped=2 (WIN-019, WIN-013 already re-reviewed since audit).
our_argument_summary: WIN-009 ('Telluric ~12 Hz peak') is Refuted by Data. The 11–12 Hz region is an inter-harmonic minimum between the Schumann fundamental (7.83 Hz) and first harmonic (14.3 Hz), not a peak. The robust first harmonic at 14.3 Hz falsifies any 'resonance ceiling below that frequency.' Independently fails the post-hoc test: monitor.py Domain 16 hardcodes both predicted and observed values with no live data fetch. The c6-eligible change is the ISS-1993 patch adding the full measured-harmonic enumeration (7.83/14.3/20.8/27.3/33.8 Hz) directly into detail_evidence, removing the c4/c5 standalone-readability deficit.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 16 of the dome's monitor.py (named 'Telluric Cutoff' in 08-live-power.txt) covers both WIN-008 (11.7 Hz cutoff) and WIN-009 (12 Hz peak literature claim). Per c5 analysis (which I am inheriting — raw-text/monitor.py source is not present in current clone), the domain hardcodes predicted ≈ 11.788 Hz and observed ≈ 11.787 Hz as static constants. No API call. No live MT/ELF data fetch. pass=True is the literal output of comparing two strings of constants.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Fully hardcoded. The source raw-text/02-wins.txt WIN-009 entry says 'Multiple independent MT literature sources. Telluric ~12 Hz peak consistent across independent datasets' — this is narrative description, not a derivation from dome parameters. The numerical match is asserted, not computed at runtime.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None visible for this domain — no tolerance to widen because there is no live data.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. WIN-008's detail_evidence carries the full Domain 16 code analysis (predicted=11.788, observed=11.7869, dome formula f=c/(2D) reproducing 11.787 Hz only when D ≈ Earth's polar diameter 12,713.6 km). WIN-009 inherits this via cross-link, which c5 confirmed is a defensible factoring. Note: I could not directly re-verify against raw-text/monitor.py because that file is not present in the current clone (raw-text/ contains only 01-home.txt through 08-live-power.txt). This is a limitation of the current evidence base — inheriting from c5/c4 which had access to the source.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Domain 16 in 08-live-power.txt is the narrative description of the monitor's telluric-cutoff check. Per prior cycles' direct examination of monitor.py (not re-verifiable in current clone), both predicted and observed values are static constants. The dome's own home page (raw-text/01-home.txt:10) lists 'Schumann resonance data' among its data sources but the WIN-009 specific check has no live fetch.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Inter-harmonic spectral minima between Schumann modes are predicted by standard spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity physics (Chave & Jones 2012, cited in detail_evidence). The dome relabels the gap minimum as a 'peak' — same numerical region, same underlying physics, opposite interpretation. WIN-029 explicitly cites the Schumann eigenfrequency formula f_n = c·sqrt(n(n+1))/(2πR) and notes that loss corrections (Sentman 1990, Mushtak & Williams) account for the deviation from the idealized progression.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Measured Schumann harmonics (7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, 33.8 Hz) and the inter-harmonic minimum near 11 Hz have been characterized in published MT/ELF literature for decades. The dome's source claim (raw-text/02-wins.txt:78) cites 'Multiple independent MT literature sources' — by definition pre-existing publications. No independent timestamp anchors the prediction prior to data adoption.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The dome's f = c/(2D) formula reproduces ≈11.787 Hz only when D ≈ 12,716 km (with c=299792.458) or 12,725.9 km (with c=300,000) — i.e., approximately Earth's polar diameter (12,713.5 km). It does NOT reproduce 11.787 Hz from any dome-specific parameter: firmament height 9,086 km → 16.50 Hz, disc radius 20,015 km → 7.49 Hz, 2× firmament height → 8.25 Hz. The derivation silently substitutes a globe parameter (Earth's polar diameter) to hit the target. WIN-008's detail_evidence carries the full numerical comparison.

holes_found.description: The newly added sentence reads 'all four harmonics above the fundamental fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula.' The phrase 'fit within' implies precise correspondence, but the measured harmonics deviate from the idealized n(n+1) progression by 5.4% (n=2: predicted 13.56, measured 14.30), 8.5% (n=3: 19.18 vs 20.80), 10.3% (n=4: 24.76 vs 27.30), and 11.5% (n=5: 30.33 vs 33.80). This deviation is well-understood (cavity loss + dispersion; Sentman 1990, Mushtak & Williams 2002) but the present wording could be quote-mined by a dome defender as 'your own text admits the harmonics don't precisely fit.' WIN-029 already handles this explicitly by citing the loss-correction literature.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to 'follow the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity progression' or 'are predicted by spherical cavity physics with standard loss corrections.' Either phrasing preserves the argument while preempting the quote-mine. Alternatively, delete the clause entirely — the core falsification ('none corresponds to 11.7 Hz') stands without it.

holes_found.description: raw-text/monitor.py is not present in the current dome-curmudgeon-clone (raw-text/ contains only 01-home.txt through 08-live-power.txt). The c5 code-analysis claims about monitor.py Domain 16 hardcoding (predicted ≈ 11.788, observed ≈ 11.787) cannot be re-verified in this cycle's clone. This is a procedural limitation — the prior reviews (c3, c4, c5) appear to have had access to monitor.py raw text. Either monitor.py was removed from raw-text/ at some point or the poller is no longer mirroring it. If a future cycle needs to verify Domain 16 code claims it will hit the same blocker.
holes_found.recommendation: Flag for tinker/poller to confirm whether raw-text/monitor.py is being intentionally omitted or has fallen out of sync. Not blocking on WIN-009 specifically — the substantive code claims are well-established across c3/c4/c5 — but the audit trail thins out for future verifications.

stronger_arguments.description: Could add a numerical anchor for the inter-harmonic minimum's location. The geometric mean of 7.83 and 14.3 is 10.58 Hz; arithmetic mean is 11.07 Hz. The dome's claimed 11.787 Hz sits in the upper half of the gap, closer to the n=2 harmonic. This places the dome's 'peak' inside the inter-harmonic minimum but slightly off-center — useful precision because it shows the dome value is not just 'somewhere in the gap' but specifically near the upper shoulder of the n=1 to n=2 trough. Not a defect of current text; an enrichment opportunity if a future polish pass goes here.
sources: computed by curmudgeon c6: GM(7.83, 14.3)=10.58, AM=11.07

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome's WIN-009 source claim (raw-text/02-wins.txt:78) is correct that the 11–12 Hz region is spectrally distinctive across MT datasets — it consistently appears in published telluric spectra. The c5 kernel_of_truth (data/wins.json:WIN-009.detail_evidence) already grants this: the cavity's standing-wave structure produces less coherent energy at the inter-harmonic gap than at the resonant modes on either side. A naive ELF spectrogram would absolutely show 'something' near 12 Hz.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being right that 11–12 Hz is spectrally distinctive does not save the claim that it is a 'peak.' The dome model has the sign inverted: a power minimum (data/wins.json:WIN-008.detail_evidence) is the opposite of a resonance peak. The newly-added enumeration in detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-009.detail_evidence, ISS-1993 patch closed 2026-05-14) makes this more concrete: 7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, and 33.8 Hz are where energy concentrates — the cavity has no harmonic at 11.7 Hz, so any 'peak' there cannot be a cavity mode. The kernel of truth (something is happening at 11–12 Hz) survives intact under standard physics; the dome's specific claim (it is a peak attributable to dome geometry) does not.

deeper_analysis:
ISS-1993 (the c4-flagged carry-over for standalone readability) was closed via EXP-369 P0 patch on 2026-05-14. The drift-audit picked this up as a +23.8% detail_evidence growth — the only field that changed. Comparing the c5 fingerprint (detail_evidence_length: 850) to the current (1052), the +202 chars correspond exactly to the new sentence 'Measured Schumann harmonics span 7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, and 33.8 Hz; all four harmonics above the fundamental fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula, and none corresponds to 11.7 Hz.' Substantively this is the patch that c4/c5 explicitly recommended — standalone-readability deficit is now resolved.

Quantitative verification (Lens C) confirms: the measured harmonics deviate from the idealized n(n+1) Schumann progression by 5–11%, with deviation growing at higher modes (Sentman 1990 loss model). The new clause 'fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula' is therefore loose — flagged as minor hole #1 with recommended softening. The dome's f=c/(2D)=11.787 Hz reverse-solves to D≈12,716 km (c=299792.458) or 12,726 km (c=300,000), both within ~12 km of Earth's polar diameter (12,713.5 km) and far from any dome-specific scale.

Cross-WIN consistency (Lens B): WIN-009 is compatible with WIN-008 (which cross-links it for the full code-analysis context), WIN-002 (Schumann damping — discusses the same idealized vs measured frequency gap), WIN-029 (which explicitly cites the spherical-shell eigenfrequency formula and acknowledges the loss correction needed to get 7.83 Hz from the idealized 10.6 Hz). No contradictions.

Advocate mode (Lens A): a dome defender could attack with 'your own model has empirical fudge factors too (Sentman loss corrections), and our f=c/(2D) gives 11.787 to 0.01%.' Counter: the dome's D=12,716 km is Earth's polar diameter — the dome silently imports a globe parameter to hit its prediction, which is exactly the dome's pattern across WIN-008/029/038. Defense survives at 2/5 — coherent but easily refuted by direct examination of where the dome's D comes from. The wording fix recommended in hole #1 would preempt the cleanest version of this attack.

Minor procedural concern (hole #2): raw-text/monitor.py is missing from this clone. The Domain 16 hardcoding claim is well-established across c3/c4/c5 prior cycles but cannot be re-verified directly. Flagged for tinker/poller audit; not blocking on WIN-009.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Three-part defense from a dome defender who has read our review carefully: (1) 'You invoke spherical-cavity physics to dismiss our peak, but your own WIN-029 review acknowledges the cavity formula needs empirical loss corrections (Sentman 1990) to recover the measured 7.83 Hz from the theoretical 10.6 Hz. Your model has its own fudge factors. Meanwhile our f=c/(2D)=11.787 matches the observation to 0.01% with no corrections.' (2) 'Your appeal to inter-harmonic minimum assumes ONE specific cavity model dominates ELF spectra. But local geological inhomogeneities — Moho discontinuities, crustal magma chambers — produce real telluric peaks at non-Schumann frequencies. Why is your global cavity coupling more parsimonious than ours?' (3) 'You cite Chave & Jones (2012) as a textbook reference but don't point to a paper that specifically targeted 11.7 Hz and reported no peak. Show me the null measurement.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Soften the new 'fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula' clause to 'are predicted by spherical cavity physics with standard loss corrections (Sentman 1990; Mushtak & Williams 2002)' — this preempts the 'your model has fudge factors too' attack by acknowledging the loss correction openly rather than hiding it. The dome's f=c/(2D)→D≈12,716 km argument is already defeated in WIN-008's detail_evidence (silent globe-parameter import); cross-linking that explicitly in WIN-009 would also help. Hole #2 (geological resonance attack) is weak because the dome's own claim is 'consistent across independent datasets' i.e. universal, not local.

claims_checked: Fit to idealized n(n+1) progression (the 'fit within' clause in new text)
Dome's f = c/(2D) ≈ 11.787 Hz → reverse-solved D
Inter-harmonic minimum location (geometric mean and arithmetic mean of 7.83 and 14.3)
quantitative_verification.computations: Used c=299792.458 km/s and c=300000 km/s as bracketing values of light speed. Idealized n(n+1)-progression frequencies (normalized to f_1=7.83): n=2 predicted 13.56 (measured 14.30, +5.4%); n=3 predicted 19.18 (measured 20.80, +8.5%); n=4 predicted 24.76 (measured 27.30, +10.3%); n=5 predicted 30.33 (measured 33.80, +11.5%). Reverse-solving 11.787 = c/(2D): D = 12,716.55 km (c=299792.458) or 12,725.88 km (c=300,000), both within ~13 km of Earth's polar diameter (12,713.5 km). Inter-harmonic minimum between fundamental and first harmonic: GM(7.83, 14.3) = 10.58 Hz; AM = 11.07 Hz; dome's 11.787 Hz sits in the upper half of the gap.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The newly-added clause 'all four harmonics above the fundamental fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula' overstates the precision of the fit: measured harmonics deviate from the idealized n(n+1) progression by 5–11%, with deviation growing at higher modes. The cavity physics is correct; the harmonics ARE spherical cavity modes; but they require standard loss-correction literature (Sentman 1990) to fit precisely. Flagged as hole #1 (minor) with softening recommendation. None of the other harmonic values, Earth-diameter comparisons, or inter-harmonic minimum computations had any discrepancy with our text.

citations_verified: doi.org/10.1017/CBO9781139020138 (Chave & Jones 2012, The Magnetotelluric Method — verified in c3/c4/c5 and consistent with current usage)
Measured Schumann harmonic values (7.83/14.3/20.8/27.3/33.8 Hz — standard textbook values, consistent across Cherry 2002, Williams 1992, Schlegel & Füllekrug 2002)
citations_unchecked: john09289.github.io/predictions (dome author's site — link target referenced for 'dome author's own definition' of prediction; same as c5, not re-verified this cycle)

Sentman 1990 / Mushtak & Williams 2002 (loss-corrected Schumann cavity papers — only invoked by curmudgeon analysis here, not currently cited in WIN-009 prose; would need to be added if the hole #1 recommendation is taken)

summary_for_decider: WIN-009.c6 = change-detected verification of ISS-1993 patch. Patch landed cleanly: detail_evidence now carries the full Schumann harmonic enumeration (7.83/14.3/20.8/27.3/33.8 Hz) for standalone readability — addressing the c4/c5 carry-over deficit. Verdict 'Refuted by Data' stands. Two minor holes found: (1) new clause 'fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula' is loose given the 5–11% deviation of measured harmonics from the idealized progression — recommend softening (specific replacement text provided in hole #1 recommendation); (2) procedural — raw-text/monitor.py not present in current clone, flagging for tinker/poller audit (not blocking WIN-009). Advocate mode: defense survives 2/5; the recommended wording softening would preempt the cleanest dome-defender attack ('your model has fudge factors too'). Lens B (cross-WIN consistency) all clean. Lens C (quantitative verification) confirms all values except the 'fit within' overclaim.
